description: Fraudulent SMS or messaging-app texts that impersonate banks, government agencies, delivery services, utilities or trusted businesses to steal credentials, OTPs, payment details or money.
recommended_actions: Do not click links in suspicious SMS or chat messages.
Do not share OTPs, UPI PINs, passwords, Aadhaar, PAN or card details.
Verify the message only through official apps, websites or customer care numbers.
Report financial fraud immediately by calling 1930.
Preserve the SMS, sender ID, phone number, URL, screenshots and transaction details.

rules.name: Suspicious Link in SMS
rules.explanation: The message contains a link or shortened URL that may lead to a fake login page, malware download or payment trap.
examples: Your bank account will be blocked. Click this link to verify now.
Update your details immediately: https://example-bank-verify.com

rules.name: Bank Account Blocking Threat
rules.explanation: Creates fear of bank account, debit card or net banking suspension to pressure the victim into clicking a link or sharing information.
examples: Your SBI account will be blocked today. Update KYC now.
Debit card suspended. Click the link to reactivate.

rules.name: KYC Update SMS
rules.explanation: Uses fake KYC expiry or verification notices, common in India for bank, wallet and UPI impersonation.
examples: Your Paytm KYC is pending. Complete now to avoid wallet suspension.
Bank KYC expired. Verify details using the link.

rules.name: OTP or Verification Code Request
rules.explanation: The text attempts to obtain an OTP or verification code through SMS, chat or follow-up call.
examples: Reply with OTP to complete account verification.
Our officer will call. Please confirm the OTP received.

rules.name: UPI Refund or Cashback Link
rules.explanation: Promises refunds, rewards or cashback to lure the victim into opening a payment link, approving a collect request or entering credentials.
examples: Your UPI refund is pending. Click to receive money.
Cashback of Rs 499 credited. Claim now using the link.

rules.name: Electricity Bill Disconnection Threat
rules.explanation: Impersonates electricity boards or bill payment services and threatens disconnection to force urgent payment or data sharing.
examples: Dear customer, your electricity will be disconnected tonight. Pay bill now.
Bijli bill not updated. Contact officer immediately.

rules.name: Courier or Parcel Delivery Scam
rules.explanation: Impersonates courier, postal or customs services to collect small payments, card details or identity documents.
examples: India Post: Your parcel is held due to incomplete address. Update now.
Delivery failed. Pay Rs 5 to reschedule your courier.

rules.name: Traffic Challan or Government Fine
rules.explanation: Uses fake traffic challan, court or government fine messages to push the victim to a fraudulent payment page.
examples: Your e-challan is pending. Pay immediately to avoid court action.
Parivahan notice: traffic fine unpaid. Click to settle.

rules.name: PAN Aadhaar or Tax Verification
rules.explanation: Impersonates income tax, PAN or Aadhaar services to steal identity details or banking credentials.
examples: PAN Aadhaar linking failed. Update now to avoid penalty.
Income tax refund approved. Verify bank account using the link.

rules.name: APK or Remote Access App Link
rules.explanation: The message asks the victim to install an APK or remote access app, which can enable device takeover or credential theft.
examples: Install this APK to update KYC.
Download customer support app and share the code.

rules.name: Card or Net Banking Credential Request
rules.explanation: Attempts to collect payment card, ATM PIN or net banking credentials through a text message or linked form.
examples: Verify your debit card number and CVV to unblock account.
Enter net banking password to receive refund.

rules.name: Urgent Final Warning
rules.explanation: Uses urgency, deadlines or penalties to reduce verification and push the victim into quick action.
examples: Final notice: verify within 24 hours or services will stop.
Act now to avoid penalty.
